title: Hymenophyllum polyanthos
taxonomy: Hymenophyllum polyanthos is classified as a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is part of the order Hymenophyllales. The order Hymenophyllales is within the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, which consists of ferns. The division Pteridophyta is a group of non-vascular and vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Hymenophyllum polyanthos, also known as the Polyanthos fern or Polynesian button fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, characterized by its unique, button-like leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Hymenophyllum polyanthos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
